title: Tea on Cotton
timeEstimate: 10–30 minutes (fresh); aged adds a peroxide cycle
stainChemistry: Tea is a classic tannin stain: tea browning pigments (oxidized catechin polymers) + general polyphenols. Black tea has the heaviest tannin load from oxidation during processing; green and white teas are lighter; herbal teas vary depending on ingredients. Polyphenols bond to cellulose through grip. Fresh tea rinses easily; aged tea oxidizes to a deeper brown. If the tea had MILK in it, treat as a combination stain — protein first — similar to coffee-cream-cotton. This card is straight tea. Cotton tolerates acid-side spotters, NSD, 3% H₂O₂, and hot wash.
whyThisWorks: Same tannin sequence as coffee: cold flush, NSD, acid-side spotter for residual color, 3% H₂O₂ for color, hot wash. The novelty versus coffee is that tea is generally lighter — most fresh spills don't need the peroxide step. Aged black tea approaches coffee in difficulty.
defaultAssumption: Most tea spills are lighter than coffee — fresh tea rinses out with cold water + NSD. Aged black tea with milk adds the protein-before-heat rule; redirect to coffee-cream protocol if milk is involved.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Cold water, white cotton terry towel
spottingProtocol.instruction: Cold flush from the BACK of the fabric. Most fresh tea lifts here.
Under 30 minutes old only. Hot pour-through from the back. Skip on aged stains.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Professional tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter or PRENETT-A) — acid-side tannin treatment
spottingProtocol.instruction: For aged or black-tea stains with residual brown, acid-side spotter by dropper. Dwell 2–5 min, blot. Skip for fresh light tea.
For residual color on white cotton: 3% H₂O₂ drop-by-drop. Skip on dark cotton.

steps: 4. Aged / black tea: acid-side tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter / PRENETT-A).
products: Professional tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter or PRENETT-A) — aged / black tea
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold through spotting; hot wash post-chemistry
warnings: Never use hot water on aged tea — sets tannin
If tea had milk / cream, use coffee-cream-cotton protocol — protein first

homeSolutions.instruction: Tea lifts easier than coffee. Hold the BACK of the stained area under cold running water for 30 seconds. Cold only.

If the tea had milk or cream in it (English breakfast, chai, milk tea), use the coffee-cream-cotton protocol — protein from the milk has to be digested before heat touches it. Plain tea: continue below.
Drop of Dawn worked into the spot with a fingertip for a minute. Rinse cool.

homeSolutions.agent: White Vinegar Dab for Residual Brown
homeSolutions.instruction: Mix 1 teaspoon white vinegar with 3 tablespoons cool water. Dab on the stain, let sit 3 min, blot.
For stubborn brown on white cotton: dab 3% drugstore hydrogen peroxide, let bubble 3–5 min, blot. Skip on dark cotton.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach or ammonia.

homeSolutions.agent: Hot Wash, Inspect Before the Dryer
homeSolutions.instruction: Launder hot per care label. Inspect BEFORE the dryer.
Tea on cotton almost never needs a pro. Escalate only if the stain has been hot-dried and home round didn't clear it.

steps: 1. Tea WITH MILK? Use coffee-cream-cotton — different chemistry.
2. Cold water flush from the BACK.
4. Diluted white vinegar for residual brown.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if tea has been hot-dried and home round didn't clear it.
warnings: Tea with milk = different protocol (protein first)
Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach or ammonia

neverDo: Never use hot water on aged tea — sets tannin
Never treat tea-with-milk as plain tea — protein needs enzyme first
fiberSensitivities: Cotton tolerates NSD, acid-side spotters, 3% H₂O₂, hot wash
Dyed cotton: skip or inside-seam-test peroxide

materialWarnings: Hot water only for fresh — sets aged tannin

escalation.when: Tea heat-set by iron or dryer and home round didn't clear it.
escalation.whatToTell: "Tea on cotton — [black / green / with milk], age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with Dawn + vinegar + 3% H₂O₂."

contextQuestions: Black, green, white, or herbal tea?
Is the cotton white, light, or dark/dyed?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Black tea approaches coffee in tannin load. Milk in the tea changes the whole protocol — use coffee-cream-cotton.
